topic: Two coworkers discover they went to the same summer camp
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara was meticulously sorting through old vacation photos when her thumb snagged on a faded picture of a muddy lake. A jolt went through her, a sudden, bright warmth spreading across her chest. She zoomed in, noticing a crudely drawn canoe on the water. Then, a name: "Camp Whispering Pines, '98." Her breath hitched. She frantically scrolled through her phone contacts, a wide, excited grin stretching across her face. She had to find someone. She typed in a vague query, her fingers flying across the screen with an energy she hadn't felt all week. The anticipation made her bounce in her chair, tapping her foot rapidly against the floorboards.

[story 2]

Mark was sketching in his notebook during his lunch break, the sun warm on his neck. He was doodling abstract shapes when he saw it—a grainy photograph of a campfire under a canopy of tall pine trees. His eyes widened, and a slow, delighted smile spread across his features. "No way," he murmured to himself, leaning closer to the screen. He immediately started cross-referencing the details, his movements quick and eager. A bubbling sensation rose in his throat. He felt a sudden, irresistible urge to text someone, to see if this shared memory was real. He started tapping out a message, his energy vibrating just beneath his skin.

[story 3]

"Seriously? You were at Camp Whispering Pines?" Chloe exclaimed, her voice bright with incredulity as she looked up from her laptop. A sudden, bubbling laugh escaped her lips, a sound full of sheer delight. She didn't stop smiling, her eyes sparkling as she gestured emphatically with her hands. "I thought I was the only one who remembered that specific counselor!" She leaned back in her chair, a feeling of buoyant lightness lifting her spirits. She started pacing the small space between her desk and the window, her movements light and unrestricted, clearly thrilled by the unexpected connection.
